title: Darklight Memento Mori - Journey and Settlement Phases
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=v1J7bERLirA

Daniel (@dungeondive) opens up the two phases of **Darklight** that no other YouTuber had filmed by 2019 — the **journey** between adventures and the **settlement** itself — using two heroes (Black Knight Ballastgo, freshly post-first-quest; Outcast Predator Saponatok, mid-second-level).

Journey first: pick a settlement size — hamlet (3 days), village (5), town (8), each giving more chances to roll up the special location you actually need. Each day you trade between accursed, draw a **journey card** (a family on the road tests charisma; *kindness* lets farmers reward you with crowns and severe-wound recovery; *agony* asks for a mind test among the skull-fields), then roll the **dread wheel** — doubles tick a marker toward space 7, where the Grim Warden appears and the hazard chart begins killing characters outright. Severe wounds, insanity and dread accumulate independently.

Settlement is location-by-location: roll the settlement's name on a chart, then each accursed visits one location per day. He levels Ballastgo at the **Accursed Guild** (30 souls + a choice of attribute or +4 HP/+1 sanity) and haggles a longsword at the **Marketplace** (1d6 per settlement level + charisma vs. each item's availability score). Locations persist between visits.

House-ruled throughout: tests pass on 5s and 6s, not just 6s.

----

The journey-and-settlement metagame is the load-bearing column of Warhammer-Quest-derived crawls. Does this look like enough metagame for repeat play, or do you want more special locations and journey-card variety before committing?
